name: QQE Stiffness Conviction Momentum
edge_hypothesis: QQE (Quantitative Qualitative Estimation) smooths RSI to eliminate noise — when QQE > 0, underlying momentum is genuinely bullish, not just a random RSI spike. STIFFNESS measures how consistently price stays above its moving average (0-100 scale). High stiffness = price is RELIABLY directional, not oscillating. Neither indicator has EVER been tested. The thesis: when smoothed momentum (QQE) and behavioral consistency (STIFFNESS) agree with trend structure (EMA_50), the signal has triple-confirmed conviction. This should work across regimes because it measures signal QUALITY rather than regime type.

variants.name: qqe_stiff_1h_8to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: QQE > 0 filters out noisy RSI signals. STIFFNESS > 60 means price has been above its MA for 12+ of the last 20 bars — consistent bullish behavior, not a one-bar spike. Combined with close > EMA_50 trend filter, this is a high-conviction directional entry. No regime gate — the conviction filters themselves act as quality control.

variants.name: qqe_stiff_4h_10to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: 4h QQE + STIFFNESS should produce fewer but higher-quality signals. 4h STIFFNESS > 60 means multi-day consistent behavior — very high conviction. 10:1 R:R captures larger moves. If this generates trades, it tests whether conviction-based filtering outperforms regime-based gating.

variants.name: qqe_stiff_1h_12to1
variants.edge_hypothesis: 12:1 R:R with tighter STIFFNESS gate (70/30 vs 60/40). STIFFNESS > 70 = 14+ of 20 bars consistently directional — extreme conviction filter. Fewer entries but each should be a high-probability trend continuation. Tests whether ultra-high conviction + ultra-high R:R can ACCEPT like RSI slingshot 12:1 did.
